briefing_memo: Dalian Enterprises received over $200 million in federal contracts since 2015, including a $7.9 million ArriveCan contract, but total federal funding to date is not specified in available records. Treasury Board suspended all contracts with Dalian in February 2024 (current-state action) after discovering its president David Yeo was simultaneously a Department of National Defence employee, and the company subsequently failed Indigenous procurement compliance audits for not meeting ownership, control, and Indigenous content criteria (theglobeandmail.com and cbc.ca, February–May 2024). The company states that Yeo filed appropriate conflict-of-interest disclosures, resigned as director/officer, placed shares in a blind trust, and agreed not to participate in DND work before becoming an employee (winnipeg.citynews.ca, March 2024). Given the current contract suspension, recent compliance failures, and that remediation claims have not been independently verified or resulted in reinstatement, recommend maintaining suspension pending completion of conflict-of-interest investigation and independent audit of Indigenous procurement eligibility.

actions.title: Conduct enhanced conflict-of-interest review
actions.rationale: Multiple HIGH-severity reports indicate a public servant's company received over $200M in contracts while employed, with auditor general firing staff over undeclared contracts and failure to meet Indigenous procurement terms.

actions.title: Verify Indigenous procurement compliance for all active contracts
actions.rationale: Audits found contractor failed to meet terms of Indigenous procurement program in ArriveCan work, requiring verification of compliance across portfolio.

actions.title: Review conflict-of-interest protocols and documentation
actions.rationale: Contractor stated conflict concerns were addressed in March 2024 remediation, warranting verification that protocols are now documented and followed.
